title: Goniopteris stolzeana
taxonomy: Goniopteris stolzeana is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically belonging to the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. It is further classified under the class Polypodiopsida, an order of vascular plants characterized by the production of sporangia. Within the order Polypodiales, Goniopteris stolzeana is placed under the family Dryopteridaceae, a family of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Goniopteris is part of the family Dryopteridaceae, with species like Goniopteris stolzeana being part of this group. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and phylogenetic relationships.

intro: Goniopteris stolzeana is a species of fern, specifically a type of polypody fern, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Goniopteris stolzeana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory infections, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
